title: dryopteris enneaphylla var. toyamae
intro: Dryopteris enneaphylla var. toyamae, also known as the Toyama five-leaved fern, is a type of flowering plant in the Dryopteridaceae family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and soothe skin conditions. The herbal preparations of Dryopteris enneaphylla var. toyamae typically involve making teas, infusions, or decoctions from the leaves of the plant.
